interpretation_summary: The replication followed the preregistered plan: the raw experimental data were re-analysed in Python, reproducing the hierarchical linear model that tests whether downstream players invest more when they received a higher share of the resource in the previous round. The focal interaction coefficient (position × lagged share) was positive and highly significant (coef = 4.52, SE = 0.48, p≈3.7e-21), closely matching and even exceeding the magnitude of the original estimate (3.836, SE = 0.836, p<.01). Thus the computational replication corroborates the original finding with greater precision, indicating strong fidelity to the theoretical claim.

fidelity_assessment.method_alignment: Full alignment: All preprocessing steps, variable constructions, round exclusions, and the mixed-effects specification with a random intercept for group match the preregistration and the original Stata code; analysis was translated verbatim into Python.

deviations.issue_description: Model fitted with REML in statsmodels instead of ML/REML choice in original Stata ‘xtmixed’.
deviations.impact: Low: REML affects variance components but fixed-effect estimates are virtually identical; significance and direction unchanged.

results_comparison.hypothesis_tested: Downstream (larger position value) players invest more in the shared infrastructure when they received a higher share of downloads in the previous round (positive pstn × shr interaction).
results_comparison.original_results: Coefficient for pstn×shr = 3.836, SE = 0.836, p < .01 (Table 8, column 3, Janssen et al. 2011).
results_comparison.replication_results: Coefficient for pstn×shr = 4.520, SE = 0.479, p ≈ 3.7 × 10⁻²¹ (statsmodels MixedLM, rounds 3-12, n = 840).
results_comparison.overall_answer: Yes – the replication meets the preregistered criteria (positive sign and p < 0.05) and yields a similar or stronger effect size.

replication_report: Replication successful: Interaction between position and lagged share remains positive and highly significant (β≈4.52, p<0.001), consistent with original estimate (β≈3.84).
notes: Because the replication reused the exact dataset, external validity was not reassessed. Future work could test the interaction in new samples or with ML vs REML estimation sensitivity.
